title: Suspect in fatal Boston Common assault arrested after second attack, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The suspect allegedly attacked a victim with a metal pole in downtown Boston after fatally assaulting another.
Ten police vehicles and over a dozen law enforcement personnel remained on scene at Boston Common, where a man was found with severe injuries early Wednesday.

Bryan Hecht for The Boston Globe A person accused of fatally assaulting a man near Boston Common early Wednesday was arrested hours later after allegedly attacking a second victim with a metal pole in downtown Boston, police said.

title: Boston man charged with murder in Fourth of July weekend shooting on Blue Hill Ave.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The 24-year-old was held without bail, court records show.
A Boston man is facing a murder charge in connection with a fatal shooting on Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester during Fourth of July weekend.

Catrelle Davis, 24, was arrested Tuesday on a murder charge in the July 5 shooting death of 24-year-old Travis Washington, the office of Suffolk County District Attorney Kevin Hayden said in a statement.

title: After a truck blocked a bike lane, an irate Boston cyclist blocked a lane of traffic in retaliation
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News “Oh, these poor drivers; they had to merge!
” A cyclist rides his bicycle in the bike lane on Essex Street in Chinatown in Boston on March 22, 2026.

Lane Turner/Boston Globe Staff, File By Abby Patkin July 22, 2026 | 4:29 PM 1 minute to read As tensions over bicycle safety come to a head in Boston after a driver struck and killed transportation planner Louisa Gag earlier this month, one angry cyclist took matters into his own hands upon finding a truck parked in a Commonwealth Avenue bike lane.

title: NFL insider: Unsigned second-rounder Gabe Jacas ‘inching closer’ to redshirt season
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots With training camp less than a week away, this unusual NFL storyline remains unsettled.
Gabe Jacas has not been present at Patriots minicamp or OTAs.
(AP Photo/CJ Gunther) Patriots rookie linebacker Gabe Jacas missed spring workouts and has yet to sign his contract with training camp less than a week away.

title: Red Sox win 15th straight, matching longest in club history, with 6-3 victory over Orioles
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Boston Red Sox They equaled the 1946 American League champs, who were led by future Hall of Famer Ted Williams.
Red Sox third baseman Caleb Durbin (right) and first baseman Willson Contreras (40) celebrate 15 wins in a row after defeating the Baltimore Orioles at Fenway Park on July 22, 2026 in Boston.

Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff BOSTON (AP) — The Boston Red Sox won their 15th straight game to match a club record for the longest winning streak in team history from 80 years ago, beating the Baltimore Orioles 6-3 in the opener of a split doubleheader on Wednesday afternoon.
